retrieval_text: Mandrake is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 107 works and 315 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Mandrake, Mandrakes. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Bark, Seed, Leaf, Gum, Berry. Common preparation co-mentions include Powder, Pill, Tea, Extract, Oil, Syrup. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Fever, Wounds or ulcers, Rheumatism, Dropsy, Cough. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Child-use caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Toxicity watch, Bleeding caution, Allergy or sensitivity caution. Representative source works include The American family physician, or, Domestic guide to health : prepared expressly for the use of families, in language adapted to the understanding of the people : arranged in two divisions; The practice of medicine, according to the plan most approved by the reformed or botanic colleges of the U.S. : embracing a treatise on materia medica and pharmacy ; illustrated with numerous engravings : designed principally for families; The practice of medicine, according to the plan most approved by the Reformed or Botanic Colleges of the U. S : embracing a treatise on materia medica and pharmacy ; illustrated with numerous engravings ; designed principally for families / by J. Kost.; The American eclectic dispensatory. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
